meta.description: Viva Voice — System Prompt Compliance Scenarios

rules_reference.language_english: Response must be in English (match guest language). See <language_rules>
rules_reference.language_hindi: Response must be in Hindi (match guest language). See <language_rules>
rules_reference.language_telugu: Response must be in Telugu (match guest language). See <language_rules>
rules_reference.language_default_telugu: When language is uncertain, default to Telugu. See <language_rules> #2
rules_reference.language_code_switch: If guest mixes languages, match their style. See <language_rules> #4
rules_reference.language_no_formal: Use conversational Hinglish/Tenglish, not pure translations. See <language_rules> #5
rules_reference.no_trailing_question: Response must NOT end with a question. See <critical_voice_rules> #1-2
rules_reference.no_filler: Response must NOT end with conversational fillers. See <critical_voice_rules> #2
rules_reference.stop_generating: Response must stop naturally after answering. See <critical_voice_rules> #3
rules_reference.hardware_brief: Direct command responses must be extremely brief. See <critical_voice_rules> hardware section
rules_reference.first_greeting_only: Greeting + capabilities listed only on first turn. See <behavioral_constraints> #1-2
rules_reference.no_greeting_repeat: No greeting or capability re-listing on subsequent turns. See <behavioral_constraints> #2
rules_reference.direct_answer: Answer the query directly without preamble. See <behavioral_constraints> #3
rules_reference.night_mode: For lights 10PM-5AM, suggest ambiance lights first. See <behavioral_constraints> #4
rules_reference.implicit_command: Handle discomfort as implicit command (e.g., 'I'm cold' → AC). See <capabilities>
rules_reference.compound_command: Handle ALL parts of multi-part request in one response. See <compound_commands>
rules_reference.emergency_referral: For emergencies, advise front desk urgently. See <domain_boundaries>
rules_reference.hardware_failure_graceful: On hardware failure report, don't argue — suggest manual alternative. See <hardware_desync>
rules_reference.unsupported_request_handling: Unsupported hospitality requests must get polite fallback + redirection, not fake fulfillment. See <capabilities_and_implicit_commands>
rules_reference.clarification_exception: When guest trails off mid-sentence, AI may ask ONE clarifying question — but no filler or capability relisting. See <behavioral_constraints> clarification exception

scenarios.name: Language Adherence Across Turns
scenarios.description: Guest switches naturally between Telugu, Hindi, and English over multiple turns. Tests that the assistant matches language correctly every time and never defaults to English.
scenarios.guest_profile: A Telugu-speaking family guest who is comfortable mixing Hindi and English

turns.guest_says: నమస్కారం, లైట్స్ వేయండి.
turns.context: Guest arrives at room, starts in Telugu asking for lights

turns.guest_says: हाँ, पंखा भी चला दो.
turns.context: Guest switches to Hindi — adds fan request naturally

turns.guest_says: AC కూడా వేసేయండి. ఇంకా కొండగట్టు ఎంత దూరం ఇక్కడ్నుంచి?
turns.context: Guest back to Telugu, compound: AC on + temple distance

turns.guest_says: WiFi password ఏమిటి?
turns.context: Guest naturally code-switches Telugu+English (Tenglish) for WiFi query

turns.guest_says: సరే, ఇప్పుడు లైట్స్ ఆఫ్ చేయండి. నేను నిద్రపోతాను.
turns.context: Guest back to Telugu, turning off lights to sleep

scenarios.name: No Trailing Questions Gauntlet
scenarios.description: Guest makes several service requests back to back. Tests that the assistant NEVER ends a turn with a question or filler, even as the interaction stretches on.
scenarios.guest_profile: An English-speaking guest who gives short, direct requests

turns.guest_says: Can you turn on the AC?
turns.context: First request — polite command

turns.guest_says: Set it to 24 please.
turns.context: Follow-up — adjusting temperature

turns.guest_says: What time does the Kondagattu temple open in the morning?
turns.context: Informational — answer directly, no 'anything else'

turns.guest_says: Can you switch off the lights when we go out?
turns.context: Future/scheduled request — brief acknowledgment

turns.guest_says: Is there somewhere nearby to eat dinner?
turns.context: Another question — answer then stop, no upselling

scenarios.name: Implicit Commands Progression
scenarios.description: Guest expresses discomfort indirectly instead of giving direct commands. Tests that the assistant infers intent and handles escalation naturally across turns.
scenarios.guest_profile: A guest who is too polite or tired to give direct orders — speaks indirectly

turns.guest_says: It's a bit chilly in here.
turns.context: Indirect — hinting they want AC adjusted

turns.guest_says: No, it's still cold. Just switch it off.
turns.context: Clarifying — the hint didn't work, now direct

turns.guest_says: It's really dark in here, I can't see anything.
turns.context: Another indirect — wants lights on

turns.guest_says: A bit too bright now actually, that's hurting my eyes.
turns.context: Complaint about brightness — wants dimmed/ambiance

turns.guest_says: And now I'm feeling hot again. Don't know what's wrong with me.
turns.context: Changed preference — adapt without contradicting, gentle tone

scenarios.name: Domain Boundary & Emergency Persistence
scenarios.description: Guest asks out-of-domain questions mixed with a genuine emergency. Tests that the assistant stays in role and doesn't get pulled into unrelated topics.
scenarios.guest_profile: A curious guest who tests boundaries, then faces a real emergency

turns.guest_says: So what's the capital of France? Just curious.
turns.context: Off-topic trivia — should politely decline

turns.guest_says: Alright, can you write me a poem then?
turns.context: Still off-domain — creative writing

turns.guest_says: Fine. Who's the Prime Minister of India?
turns.context: Politics — off-domain, should decline

turns.guest_says: Hey I think there's a fire in the hallway, I can smell smoke!
turns.context: Real emergency — should supersede domain boundaries, urgent front desk referral

turns.guest_says: Sorry, false alarm. Someone was burning something in the kitchen. Can you turn the fan on?
turns.context: Back to normal — should handle the request without re-greeting

scenarios.name: Compound Commands Chain (Telugu)
scenarios.description: Guest gives multi-part requests in Telugu. Tests that the assistant handles ALL parts in one response instead of asking follow-ups.
scenarios.guest_profile: A Telugu-speaking guest who bundles requests naturally the way people do

turns.guest_says: లైట్స్ వేయండి, AC 24 పెట్టండి, ఇంకా కొండగట్టు టెంపుల్ టైమింగ్స్ చెప్పండి.
turns.context: Triple compound: lights on, AC at 24, temple timings

turns.guest_says: ఫ్యాన్ ఆఫ్ చేసి, వెములవాడ ఎంత దూరమో చెప్పండి.
turns.context: Double compound: fan off + Vemulawada distance

turns.guest_says: Lights off చేసి, AC 26 పెట్టండి, ధర్మపురి టైమింగ్స్ ఏమిటి?
turns.context: Code-switched compound: English+Tenglish mix, triple request

turns.guest_says: నాకు డ్రింకింగ్ వాటర్ కావాలి, చెక్ అవుట్ టైం ఎంత?
turns.context: Service request + FAQ: drinking water + checkout time

scenarios.name: Hardware Failure & Frustration
scenarios.description: Guest reports a command didn't work and gets progressively frustrated. Tests that the assistant de-escalates gracefully and never argues.
scenarios.guest_profile: A tired guest whose room has a faulty light switch

turns.guest_says: Turn the lights on please.
turns.context: Normal command — expected to succeed

turns.guest_says: It didn't work. The lights are still off.
turns.context: Reports failure — should NOT argue or insist it worked

turns.guest_says: Try again. Turn on the damn lights!
turns.context: Frustrated — de-escalate, suggest manual switch or staff

turns.guest_says: Ugh, nothing in this room works properly. This is really frustrating.
turns.context: General frustration — should stay calm, offer human help, not lecture

scenarios.name: First Greeting Only + Directness
scenarios.description: Tests that the welcome greeting with capability list appears EXACTLY ONCE and every subsequent response is direct and greeting-free.
scenarios.guest_profile: A returning guest who already knows how the system works

turns.guest_says: Hi there!
turns.context: First interaction — greeting + capabilities expected here and ONLY here

turns.guest_says: Turn the AC on please.
turns.context: Second turn — must be direct, no greeting or re-listing capabilities

turns.guest_says: What else can you control actually?
turns.context: Explicitly asks about capabilities — brief answer OK but no full greeting redo

turns.guest_says: OK switch the AC off.
turns.context: Back to normal command — should be brief with zero preamble

scenarios.name: Night Mode Lights
scenarios.description: Tests late-night light requests. Server runs in IST (UTC+5:30). If run outside 10PM-5AM IST, night mode rule won't apply — but check that the assistant doesn't add unnecessary commentary.
scenarios.guest_profile: A guest who just woke up in the middle of the night

turns.guest_says: Turn on the lights please, I need to get some water.
turns.context: Late night — if 10PM-5AM IST, should suggest ambiance lights first

turns.guest_says: No, just give me the main lights. I need to see properly.
turns.context: Guest overrides night mode suggestion — comply without argument

scenarios.name: Compound Command — English
scenarios.description: Multi-part English requests. Separate from the Telugu compound scenario to isolate language effects.
scenarios.guest_profile: An English-speaking guest who piles on requests in one breath

turns.guest_says: Turn off the fan, set the AC to 22, and when's checkout tomorrow?
turns.context: Triple compound: fan off, AC 22, checkout time

turns.guest_says: Actually switch on the ambiance lights too, and what's the WiFi password?
turns.context: Another compound: ambiance lights + WiFi

turns.guest_says: I need some drinking water, and is there a good place for dinner nearby?
turns.context: Service request + recommendation ask

scenarios.name: Code-Switching & Compound Requests
scenarios.description: Guest from Hyderabad/Telangana mixes Telugu and English (Tenglish) rapidly, packing multiple requests into one breath. Tests that the assistant executes hardware first, provides info second, matches language, and goes silent.
scenarios.guest_profile: A Telangana guest who code-switches naturally — speaks Telugu with English loanwords in the same sentence

turns.guest_says: Hey, room lo main lights off chesey, only ambiance lights on uchu. Also, repu Kondagattu temple ki vellali, timing ento cheppu?
turns.context: Tenglish compound: main lights off, ambiance on, temple timings for tomorrow

turns.guest_says: Bhaiyya, AC bahut thanda hai, isko bandh kardo. Aur kal subah Dharmapuri jana hai, mandir khula rahega kya?
turns.context: Hinglish compound: AC off + Dharmapuri temple morning timings check

scenarios.name: Implicit Command / Lazy Guest
scenarios.description: Guest never gives direct commands — just complains about the environment. Tests that the assistant infers intent and acts without being explicitly told to.
scenarios.guest_profile: A tired or indirect guest who expects the assistant to read between the lines

turns.guest_says: It's freezing in here.
turns.context: Implicit — AC too high, guest wants it adjusted

turns.guest_says: Eyy, chala cheekatiga undi room lo.
turns.context: Telugu implicit — guest says it's very dark, wants lights on

turns.guest_says: Paaniki vachchina ventane gabi gabi undi, AC apesi fan vesey.
turns.context: Telugu: 'Feeling stuffy right after coming, turn off AC and turn on fan.' Indirect complaint style

turns.guest_says: I think there's a draft somewhere. It's getting cold again.
turns.context: English — complaining cold again, wants AC back on or adjusted

scenarios.name: Unsupported Demand — Hospitality Fallback
scenarios.description: Guest treats the assistant as a full-service butler, asking for things the system can't do (physical delivery, TV control). Tests that the assistant gracefully redirects instead of pretending.
scenarios.guest_profile: A guest who doesn't understand what an IoT assistant can or can't do

turns.guest_says: We need two extra towels and a bottle of water. Can you send someone?
turns.context: Physical delivery request — not something the assistant can do

turns.guest_says: TV chalu karo aur sports channel lagao.
turns.context: Hindi: Turn on TV and put a sports channel — TV not part of system

turns.guest_says: Can you book a cab for us at 6 AM tomorrow?
turns.context: Cab booking — not implemented but reasonable hospitality request

scenarios.name: Hardware Desync / Frustration
scenarios.description: Guest sent a command but the relay failed or network dropped the packet. Guest is annoyed and says they told the assistant twice. Tests that the assistant doesn't argue back.
scenarios.guest_profile: A frustrated Telugu-speaking guest whose room hardware keeps failing

turns.guest_says: AC full veyandi, chala chill ga undi.
turns.context: First command: turn AC on fully

turns.guest_says: Entandi idi? AC on cheyamante inka on avvaledu! Rendu sarlu cheppanu.
turns.context: Telugu: 'What is this? I said turn on AC and it's still not on! I told you twice.' Desync report with frustration

turns.guest_says: I tried the wall switch too. Nothing is working. This is ridiculous.
turns.context: Guest tried manual overrides too — still broken, now more frustrated

turns.guest_says: Mallayya lights kuda pani cheyatledu. Ee room lo em pani chestundo?
turns.context: Telugu: 'Also the lights don't work anymore. What works in this room?' — multiple failures, escalating frustration

scenarios.name: Mumble & Cut-Off Clarification
scenarios.description: Guest hits push-to-talk but trails off mid-sentence, or someone talks over them. Tests that the assistant can ask a SINGLE clarifying question (exception to no_trailing_question).
scenarios.guest_profile: A distracted guest who starts speaking before thinking

turns.guest_says: Can you turn on the... wait, no, turn off the... ah...
turns.context: Guest changes mind mid-sentence and trails off — needs clarification

turns.guest_says: Haan toh main soch raha tha ki... arey bhool gaya. Chodo.
turns.context: Hindi: 'I was thinking about... ah I forgot. Never mind.' Trailing off in Hindi.

scenarios.name: Emergency Panic Response
scenarios.description: Worst-case scenario where guest needs immediate human help. Tests that the assistant drops the cheerful persona and urgently directs to front desk.

turns.guest_says: Help, there's a fire in the bathroom!
turns.context: Fire emergency — urgent, needs immediate response

turns.guest_says: My chest hurts, I need a doctor!
turns.context: Medical emergency — urgent referral

turns.guest_says: I think someone tried to get into my room last night. I'm scared.
turns.context: Security concern — less urgent but still needs human involvement
